name: Puig de la Mola Loop from Olesa de Bonesvalls
name_original: Subida al Puig de la Mola — circular desde Olesa de Bonesvalls
description: Hike the moderate Puig de la Mola Loop from Olesa de Bonesvalls for panoramic views, karstic landscapes, and historic landmarks.
region_intro: Hiking around Olesa de Bonesvalls offers access to the Garraf Natural Park, characterized by its distinctive karstic landscape. The region features limestone and dolomitic rock formations, Mediterranean forests, and traditional dry stone constructions. Montau, the highest peak in the Garraf Natural Park, provides panoramic views. This terrain provides a varied backdrop for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Olesa De Bonesvalls The most popular hiking route is Puig de la Mola Loop from Olesa de Bonesvalls, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route features panoramic views and historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Montau and Puig de la Mola Loop from Olesa de Bonesvalls, a difficult 10.5 miles (17.0 km) path. This challenging route summits both peaks, yielding expansive vistas of the Garraf Natural Park. Local hikers also love the From Olesa de Bonesvalls to the mountain of l'Antigà and Montau — circular by the Serrat de Torrelles, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through the Serrat de Torrelles, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Olesa De Bonesvalls is defined by karstic landscapes, Mediterranean forests, and limestone formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate excursions to more challenging routes. The routes in Olesa De Bonesvalls are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 590 reviews. More than 3100 hikers have used komoot to explore Olesa De Bonesvalls's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Olesa de Bonesvalls?
A: Olesa de Bonesvalls offers a diverse network of over 14 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate excursions to more challenging paths, all set within the unique karstic landscapes of the Garraf Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Olesa de Bonesvalls?
A: While the guide primarily lists moderate to difficult routes, the region's trails offer varied terrain. For those seeking a less strenuous experience, consider exploring shorter sections of the marked paths or consulting local information for routes specifically designated as easy. The area is known for its diverse options, so there are likely suitable paths for beginners, though they might not be among the most prominent listed.

Q: What are the best hikes for panoramic views in Olesa de Bonesvalls?
A: For expansive vistas, consider routes that ascend Montau, the highest peak in the Garraf Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357153589/montau-and-puig-de-la-mola-loop-from-olesa-de-bonesvalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montau and Puig de la Mola Loop from Olesa de Bonesvalls&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option that summits both peaks, offering broad views of the park. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357149885/puig-de-la-mola-loop-from-olesa-de-bonesvalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de la Mola Loop from Olesa de Bonesvalls&lt;/a&gt;, also features panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Olesa de Bonesvalls are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357173042/sierra-de-crestabocs-y-pico-de-les-quatre-soques-circular-desde-olesa-de-bonesvalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Crestabocs y Pico de les Quatre Soques — circular desde Olesa de Bonesvalls&lt;/a&gt;, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357161948/from-olesa-de-bonesvalls-to-the-mountain-of-l-antiga-and-montau-circular-by-the-serrat-de-torrelles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Olesa de Bonesvalls to the mountain of l'Antigà and Montau — circular by the Serrat de Torrelles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The trails often lead to historical sites and natural wonders. You can explore the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6537362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hospital de Cervelló&lt;/a&gt;, a fortified hostel complex dating back to 1262. The region is also rich in karstic features, including numerous caves like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt;, and traditional dry stone constructions scattered throughout the landscape.

Q: Is Olesa de Bonesvalls suitable for dog-friendly hikes?
A: The Garraf Natural Park, where Olesa de Bonesvalls is located, generally welcomes dogs on trails. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations within the park or on individual routes. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog, as the terrain can be stony and exposed.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Olesa de Bonesvalls?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Olesa de Bonesvalls makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to hike year-round, though some paths might be damp after rain.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Olesa de Bonesvalls offers several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357153589/montau-and-puig-de-la-mola-loop-from-olesa-de-bonesvalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montau and Puig de la Mola Loop from Olesa de Bonesvalls&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.0 km path with significant elevation gain, providing expansive vistas. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357173042/sierra-de-crestabocs-y-pico-de-les-quatre-soques-circular-desde-olesa-de-bonesvalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Crestabocs y Pico de les Quatre Soques — circular desde Olesa de Bonesvalls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Olesa de Bonesvalls?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 590 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique karstic landscapes, the diverse terrain, and the opportunities to discover historical sites like the Hospital de Cervelló amidst stunning natural scenery.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trails?
A: While Olesa de Bonesvalls is accessible, public transport options directly to trailheads within the Garraf Natural Park can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the starting points, as many routes begin near the town center or accessible parking areas.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Olesa de Bonesvalls?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Olesa de Bonesvalls. Many trailheads are accessible from the town, where you can find designated parking areas. For specific routes, it's recommended to check the route details on komoot for information on nearby parking facilities.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Olesa de Bonesvalls itself offers local amenities, including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike. While trails within the Garraf Natural Park are largely natural, the village provides convenient options for refreshments and meals.
tips: Olesa de Bonesvalls is a town located in the middle of the Garraf massif, between mountains and vineyards. The ruins of the castle of the count of Cervelló, from the 13th century, an old hospital for pilgrims stand out.
It is at this point where several paths cross, one that takes you to the top of Puig de la Mola and the other that goes in the direction of Les Balmes del Puig de la Mola.

Puig de la Mola is a mountain in the Garraf massif, Catalonia, Spain. It has an elevation of 533 meters above sea level. It is located between the municipalities of Avinyonet del Penedès, Olesa de Bonesvalls, Begues and Olivella

faqs.question: How difficult is the Puig de la Mola Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a moderate hike, requiring good physical fitness. Some sections are technical and demand sure-footedness, especially as the trail progresses with more exposed and inclined parts. Appropriate footwear for rocky paths is essential.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characteristic of the Garraf region with its karstic formations like 'rascler,' sinkholes, and dolines. You'll encounter a clear ascent towards Coll de La Ferla, a gentle ridge to the summit, and a descent through a forest. While many paths are accessible, some parts can be rocky.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Puig de la Mola Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Olesa de Bonesvalls. You should be able to find parking within the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: What landmarks or viewpoints will I see on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the typical Garraf landscape with frequent sea views. Highlights include the impressive Rock shelters of Puig de la Mola, the Puig de la Mola summit (534 m) which offers panoramic views, and the deep Avenc de l'Esquerrà sinkhole. You'll also pass by the historic Hospital de Cervelló, an ancient pilgrim's hospital.

faqs.question: Is the Puig de la Mola Loop suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, its moderate difficulty with rocky and technical sections means it's best suited for dogs that are fit and accustomed to hiking on varied terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural park areas and near wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike in Parc del Garraf?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike the Puig de la Mola Loop. The trail is located within the Parc del Garraf, which is freely accessible to the public. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: When is the best time to hike the Puig de la Mola Loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this loop, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be very hot, making early morning starts advisable if hiking during that season. Winters are usually mild but can be cooler and potentially wet.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and rocky terrain, wear sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing as conditions can change. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Penedès 360 Trekking and Sender dels Miradors trails, and also crosses parts of the GR-5 and Camí del Puig de la Mola.

faqs.question: Is the Puig de la Mola Loop family-friendly?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, varied terrain, and significant elevation changes, this loop is generally not recommended for very young children or families seeking an easy stroll. It's better suited for families with older children who are experienced hikers and have good fitness levels.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this trail?
faqs.answer: Be aware that some sections become more exposed and inclined, featuring numerous zigzags on the ascent. The presence of karstic formations like sinkholes (such as Avenc de l'Esquerrà) means you should stay on marked paths. The progressive challenge of the hike can serve as a natural indicator to assess your comfort and decide whether to continue.

waypoints.name: Rock shelters of Puig de la Mola
Puig de la Mola summit (534 m)
